An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n#\n#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n#\n# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n# reconnection tests.\nKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n)\n\n# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n_DROP = object()\n\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEConnection:\n    \"\"\"An active SSE connection.\"\"\"\n    connection_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n    queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)\n    connected_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n    last_heartbeat: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEEvent:\n    \"\"\"An SSE event to broadcast.\n\n    Attributes:\n        event_type: The type of SSE event (e.g., 'result_created', 'topic_updated')\n        data: The event payload data\n        rendered_html: Optional rendered HTML for canvas injection (e.g., pre-rendered card)\n        target_session_id: Optional filter to only send to connections for this session\n        target_surface_id: Optional filter to only send to this specific surface\n        exclude_surface_id: Optional filter to exclude this surface from receiving the event\n    \"\"\"\n    event_type: str\n    data: dict\n    rendered_html: str | None = None\n    target_session_id: str | None = None\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None\n    exclude_surface_id: str | None = None\n\n\nclass SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"\n    Manages SSE connections and broadcasts events.\n\n    Events are queued per-connection and sent via SSE.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.connections: Dict[str, SSEConnection] = {}\n        self._cleanup_task: asyncio.Task | None = None\n        self._running = False\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._cleanup_task = asyncio.create_task(self._cleanup_loop())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._cleanup_task:\n            self._cleanup_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._cleanup_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n    def register(\n        self,\n        surface_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> SSEConnection:\n        \"\"\"Register a new SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        connection_id = str(uuid4())\n        connection = SSEConnection(\n            connection_id=connection_id,\n            surface_id=surface_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            surface_type=surface_type,\n        )\n        self.connections[connection_id] = connection\n        logger.info(f\"Registered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {surface_id}\")\n        return connection\n\n    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n            del self.connections[connection_id]\n\n    def drop_session(self, session_id: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Abruptly drop every live SSE stream for ``session_id``.\n\n        Pushes the ``_DROP`` sentinel onto each matching connection's queue so\n        its :meth:`event_generator` returns without emitting a ``disconnect``\n        event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. Returns the\n        number of streams signalled. Used by ``POST /api/v1/test/drop-sse`` to\n        faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop, which\n        ``context.set_offline`` cannot reproduce against a loopback server\n        (loopback connections are exempt from offline emulation — see\n        tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n        \"\"\"\n        count = 0\n        for conn in self.connections.values():\n            if conn.session_id == session_id:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(_DROP)\n                count += 1\n        if count:\n            logger.info(f\"Drop requested for session {session_id}: {count} stream(s)\")\n        return count\n\n    def heartbeat(self, connection_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Update heartbeat for a connection. Returns True if connection exists.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            self.connections[connection_id].last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    async def broadcast(self, event: SSEEvent) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast an event to relevant connections.\n\n        Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n        \"\"\"\n        sent_count = 0\n\n        for conn in list(self.connections.values()):\n            # Filter by target\n            if event.target_session_id and conn.session_id != event.target_session_id:\n                continue\n            if event.target_surface_id and conn.surface_id != event.target_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if event.exclude_surface_id and conn.surface_id == event.exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n\n            # Queue the event\n            try:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(event)\n                sent_count += 1\n            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                logger.warning(f\"Queue full for connection {conn.connection_id}, dropping event\")\n\n        return sent_count\n\n    async def event_generator(self, connection: SSEConnection):\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate SSE events for a connection.\n\n        Yields formatted SSE messages.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield self._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"connection_id\": connection.connection_id,\n                \"surface_id\": connection.surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": connection.session_id,\n            })\n\n            while True:\n                # Block on the next event, but no longer than the keepalive\n                # interval: if nothing arrives, emit a comment-line ping so the\n                # stream is never silent. A silent idle stream is indistinguish-\n                # able from a dead one to the browser — EventSource only fires\n                # ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail — so without these pings\n                # a real network drop on an idle surface leaves it showing\n                # \"Connected\" until the OS TCP keepalive times out (minutes\n                # later). The ping is a comment line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``), which\n                # EventSource ignores — it surfaces no message to the client.\n                try:\n                    event = await asyncio.wait_for(\n                        connection.queue.get(),\n                        timeout=KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS,\n                    )\n                except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n EventSource onerrors + reconnects.\n                if event is _DROP:\n                    logger.info(f\"Drop sentinel on connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    return\n\n                connection.last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n\n                # Format and yield the event\n                payload = dict(event.data)\n                if event.rendered_html is not None:\n                    payload[\"rendered_html\"] = event.rendered_html\n                yield self._format_sse(event.event_type, payload)\n\n                # Special case: disconnect event ends the stream\n                if event.event_type == \"disconnect\":\n                    logger.info(f\"Disconnect event for connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    break\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"Connection {connection.connection_id} cancelled\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.unregister(connection.connection_id)\n\n    def _format_sse(self, event_type: str, data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format an event as SSE.\"\"\"\n        return f\"event: {event_type}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n\n    async def _cleanup_loop(self):\n        \"\"\"Periodically clean up dead connections.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await asyncio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds\n\n                now = datetime.now().timestamp()\n                timeout = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n                dead_connections = [\n                    cid for cid, conn in self.connections.items()\n                    if (now - conn.last_heartbeat) > timeout\n                ]\n\n                for cid in dead_connections:\n                    logger.info(f\"Cleaning up dead connection {cid}\")\n                    # Send disconnect event before removing\n                    try:\n                        conn = self.connections[cid]\n                        conn.queue.put_nowait(SSEEvent(\n                            event_type=\"disconnect\",\n                            data={\"reason\": \"timeout\"},\n                        ))\n                    except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                        pass\n                    self.unregister(cid)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in cleanup loop: {e}\")\n\n\n# Global broadcaster instance\n_broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster | None = None\n\n\ndef get_broadcaster() -> SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n    global _broadcaster\n    if _broadcaster is None:\n        _broadcaster = SSEBroadcaster()\n    return \"heartbeat\"\n\n    # Result events\n    RESULT_CREATED = \"result_created\"\n    RESULT_UPDATED = \"result_updated\"\n\n    # Component events (Phase 2)\n    COMPONENT_UPDATED = \"component_updated\"\n\n    # Intent events\n    INTENT_PENDING = \"intent_pending\"\n    INTENT_DISPATCHED = \"intent_dispatched\"\n    INTENT_RESOLVED = \"intent_resolved\"\n\n    # Topic events\n EXCEPTION_RAISED = \"exception_raised\"\n\n    # Bead events\n    BEAD_CLOSED = \"bead_closed\"\n    BEAD_FAILED = \"bead_failed\"\n\n    # Circuit breaker events\n    TASK_STUCK = \"task_stuck\"\n    TASK_FAILED = \"task_failed\"\n\n    # Approval events (Generated-Bead Safety)\n    APPROVAL_REQUIRED = \"approval_required\"\n    APPROVAL_GRANTED = \"approval_granted\"\n    APPROVAL_DENIED =  appropriate error cards.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._broadcaster = None\n\n    def _get_broadcaster(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n Fired when ZAI proxy is down/timeout/quota at the router stage.\n        Client renders dispatch-level error card showing raw utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The original user utterance (preserved for retry)\n            intent_id: Intent ID for tracking\n            session_id: Session ID for targeting\n            error_reason: \"timeout\" | \"quota_exhausted\" | \"proxy_down\" | \"unknown_error\"\n\n        Returns:\n            Number of connections the event was sent to\n        \"\"\"\n        event_payload = RouterUnavailableEvent(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            error_reason=error_reason,\n            timestamp=datetime.now().isoformat(),\n Fetched data\n    is never discarded.\n    \"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    intent_type: str\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    fetched_context: dict  # Preserved fetch data for degraded rendering\n    error_reason: str\n    timestamp: str\n    retry_allowed: bool = True  # Retry-synthesize action reuses fetched context\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ClarificationCardEvent:\n    \"\"\"Payload for clarification_card error event.\n\n    Client renders: Clarification-style card showing the utterance\n    with an edit-and-resend action. \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._broadcaster = None\n\n    def _get_broadcaster(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n        if self._broadcaster is None:\n            self._broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n        return self._broadcaster\n\n    async def broadcast_router_unavailable(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        intent_id: str,\n       intent_id=intent_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            error_reason=error_reason,\n            timestamp=datetime.now().isoformat(),\n            retry_allowed=True,\n trace.\n\nBroadcasts SSE error events that the client templates consume to render\nappropriate error cards.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport logging\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import — LLM proxy unreachable\"\n    with the raw utterance shown so nothing is lost.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance: str\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    error_reason: str  # \"timeout\" | \"quota_exhausted\" | \"proxy_down\" | \"unknown_error\"\n    timestamp: str\n    retry_allowed: bool = True\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass AllSourcesFailedEvent:\n    \"\"\"Payload for all_sources_failed error event.\n\n    Client renders: \"No data\" error card with intent header +\n    per-source failure list. Fetched data\n    is never discarded.\n    \"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    intent_type: str\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    fetched_context: dict  # Preserved fetch data for degraded rendering\n    error_reason: str\n    timestamp: str\n    retry_allowed: bool = True  # Retry-synthesize action reuses fetched context\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ClarificationCardEvent:\n    \"\"\"Payload for clarification_card error event.\n\n    Client renders: Clarification-style card showing the utterance\n    with an edit-and-resend action. Raw model output goes to logs,\n    never the canvas.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance: str\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    parse_error: str\n    timestamp: str\n    retry_count: int  # Number of retries attempted (max 1 corrective retry)\n    raw_output_snippet: str | None = None  # First 200 chars of malformed output for logs\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ActionDesignOnlyEvent:\n    \"\"\"Payload for action_design_only event.\n\n    Client renders: Design-only card explaining that action execution\n    is not yet built, offering to requeue as a task-profile bead\n    (the reviewed-bead path that exists).\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance: str\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    timestamp: str = \"\"\n    requeue_allowed: bool = available yet.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance: str\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    timestamp: str\n\n\nclass DegradedStateHandler:\n    \"\"\"\n    Handles degraded-state error events for the failure-mode matrix.\n\n    Each failure mode broadcasts a specific SSE error event with a\n    well-defined payload that the client templates consume to render\n    appropriate error cards.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._broadcaster = None\n\n    def _get_broadcaster(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n        if self._broadcaster is None:\n            self._broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n        return self._broadcaster\n\n    async def broadcast_router_unavailable(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        intent_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        error_reason: str = \"unknown_error\",\n    ) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast router_unavailable error event.\n\n        Fired when ZAI proxy is down/timeout/quota at the router stage.\n        Client renders dispatch-level error card showing raw utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The original user utterance (preserved for retry)\n            intent_id: Intent ID for tracking\n            session_id: Session ID for targeting\n            error_reason: \"timeout\" | \"quota_exhausted\" | \"proxy_down\" | \"unknown_error\"\n\n        Returns:\n            Number of connections the event was sent to\n        \"\"\"\n        event_payload = RouterUnavailableEvent(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            error_reason=error_reason,\n            timestamp=datetime.now().isoformat(),\n            retry_allowed=True,\n        )\n\n        event = SSEEvent(\n            event_type=EventType.ROUTER_UNAVAILABLE,\n            data={\n                \"utterance\": utterance,\n                \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                \"error_reason\": error_reason,\n                \"timestamp\": event_payload.timestamp,\n                \"retry_allowed\": True,\n                \"message\": \"Router unavailable — LLM proxy unreachable\",\n            },\n            target_session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Broadcasting router_unavailable for intent {intent_id}: {error_reason}\"\n        )\n\n        return await self._get_broadcaster().broadcast(event)\n\n    async def broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        intent_type: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        failed_sources: list[dict],\n    ) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast all_sources_failed error event.\n\n        Fired when ALL fetch sources fail. Fetched data is\n        preserved and rendered under a \"summary unavailable\" banner.\n\n        Args:\n            intent_id: Intent ID for tracking\n            intent_type: Intent type string\n            session_id: Session ID for targeting\n            utterance: The original utterance\n            fetched_context: The FetchResult with preserved data\n            error_reason: Reason for synthesize failure\n\n        Returns:\n            Number of connections the event was sent to\n        \"\"\"\n        # Serialize fetch context for client rendering\n        fetch_data = {\n            \"coverage\": {\n                \"total_sources\": fetched_context.coverage.total_sources,\n                \"succeeded\": len(fetched_context.coverage.succeeded),\n                \"timed_out\": len(fetched_context.coverage.timed_out),\n                \"failed\": len(fetched_context.coverage.failed),\n            },\n            \"caveats\": fetched_context.caveats or [],\n        }\n\n \"success\":\n                sources_data[source.value] = result.data\n            else:\n                sources_data[source.value] = {\n                    \"status\": result.status,\n                    \"error\": result.error,\n                }\n\n        fetch_data[\"sources\"] = sources_data\n\n        event_payload = DegradedRawDataEvent(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            utterance=utterance,\n            fetched_context=fetch_data,\n            error_reason=error_reason,\n      },\n            target_session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Broadcasting degraded_raw_data for intent {intent_id}: {error_reason}\"\n        )\n\n Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n            }\n\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            }\n\n        try:\n            # = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    return await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                except json.JSONDecode as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(e),\n                        raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n                classification.intent_type,\n                classification.lookup_kind\n            )\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n\n            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_first_source)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n                )\n\n                # Build failed sources list for error event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\": source.value,\n                        \"status\": result.status,\n                        \"error\": result.error,\n                    })\n\n                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                }\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            try:\n                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            except Exception as synth_e:\n                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n                )\n\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    f\"Synthesize failed: {synth_e}\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n                    \"degraded\": True,\n                }\n\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification (includes lookup_kind for lookups)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": Every lookup thread MUST include a `lookup_kind` field:\n  - `\"logs\"`: Recent log output, pod logs, error messages\n  - `\"config\"`: Configuration files, deployments, env vars, ArgoCD app state\n  - `\"docs\"`: Documentation, README files, project overview (default when unspecified)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n- **clarification**: Low-confidence routing outcome requiring user input (meta-type, not dispatched)\n\n## Task-Profile Classification\n\nRoute to **task-profile** when:\n- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n\nTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n\n## Routing Logic\n\nUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n- Look for direct project name matches\n- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n\n## Segmentation Guidelines\n\n- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n\n## Confidence Threshold\n\n- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n\n## Status Intent Notes\n\n- Always include pod restart count in status results.\n\nUrgency classification rules are spliced in from `prompts/urgency.md` at call time and are hot-reloadable independently of this file.\n\nReturn ONLY the JSON array.
